mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources within Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer infrastructure as code or GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator in the default namespace.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for Grafana, defining its configuration and settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with specified settings.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana Operator installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana Operator and ensures it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n default -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=grafana-operator

```

> Verifies that the Grafana Operator pod is running.

steps.title: Create a sample Grafana Dashboard
steps.description:
This step creates a sample dashboard using a custom resource (CR) to demonstrate the functionality of the Grafana Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:
  resyncPeriod: 30s
  instanceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
EOF

```

> Creates a sample dashboard in the Grafana instance.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard

  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",

      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the Grafana instance created earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n default -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=grafana-operator

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana

```

> Check the status of the Grafana Operator pods.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Grafana CRD has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to create Grafana: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Grafana Operator is not running or has crashed.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard not appearing after creation.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not be correctly linked to the dashboard CR.

**Fix:**
Verify the labels in the Grafana instance and dashboard CR match.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to connect to Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Grafana service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana service is created and check the service type (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer).

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the command 'kubectl get grafana' returning the instance details.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
